title: althaea × cultorum
intro: Althaea × cultorum, also known as holy grass or marshmallow, is a sterile hybrid plant derived from the crossing of Althaea officinalis and Althaea rostrata. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, soothing, and demulcent effects, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The health benefits of Althaea × cultorum include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and providing relief from skin irritations and wounds. Althaea × cultorum can be prepared in various ways, including making marshmallow root tea, infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties.
